2605.23605.title: DiLaDiff: Distilled Latent-Augmented Diffusion for Language Modeling
2605.23605.paper_summary: Diffusion language models intrinsically fail to capture correlations between decoded tokens, which leads to a harsh trade-off between sampling quality and throughput. To solve this issue, we propose DiLaDiff, a variant of masked diffusion language models with three components: (1) a continuous latent space with semantic capabilities, learned by an auto-encoder fine-tuned from an existing masked diffusion language model; (2) a latent diffusion model learning the prior over the encoder distribution; (3) a consistency model distilling the learned prior into a few-step latent generative model. We show that, even without distillation, our latent-guided diffusion model outperforms the masked diffusion baseline while significantly accelerating inference. Consistency distillation further lowers the computational overhead of continuous diffusion, such that the latent is generated in negligible time compared to discrete decoding.
2605.23605.paper_summary_zh: 擴散語言模型本質上無法捕捉解碼標記之間的相關性，這導致了抽樣質量和吞吐量之間的嚴重權衡。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了DiLaDiff，一種具有三個組件的掩蔽擴散語言模型變體：(1) 一個具有語義能力的連續潛在空間，由從現有的掩蔽擴散語言模型微調的自編碼器學習；(2) 一個潛在擴散模型學習編碼器分佈的先驗；(3) 一個一致性模型將學習到的先驗提煉成幾步潛在生成模型。我們展示了，即使在沒有提煉的情況下，我們的潛在引導擴散模型也超越了掩蔽擴散基線，同時顯著加速了推理。一致性提煉進一步降低了連續擴散的計算開銷，使得潛在生成的時間與離散解碼相比幾乎可以忽略不計。
2605.23605.author: Jean-Marie Lemercier et.al.
2605.23605.authors: Jean-Marie Lemercier, Tomas Geffner, Karsten Kreis, Morteza Mardani, Arash Vahdat, Ante Jukić
